description:
You are a platform engineer reviewing daily build results for a small CI/CD proof-of-concept.  
A plain-text log has already been collected for you at:

/home/user/ci_cd/logs/build.log

Your goal: produce a tidy frequency report that shows how many times each build status appears in that log and save it to the following path (create any missing directories as you go):

/home/user/ci_cd/reports/build_status_frequency.txt

Strict requirements for the output file:

1. Each line must contain exactly two fields separated by a single space:
      <STATUS> <COUNT>
2. The STATUS strings must appear in case-sensitive alphabetical order (e.g., ABORTED before FAILED before SUCCESS).
3. There must be one line per unique status, with no extra blank lines.
4. End the file with a single trailing newline character.
5. Nothing else (timestamps, hashes, etc.) may appear in the file.

Only standard shell utilities are allowed; the simplest solution uses a short pipeline with sort and uniq (e.g., “sort … | uniq -c …”).  

When you are done, you should be able to run:
    cat /home/user/ci_cd/reports/build_status_frequency.txt
and see exactly:

ABORTED 2
FAILED 4
SUCCESS 5

(plus a final newline).

Make sure the file permissions allow read access to everyone (i.e., chmod 644 or similar).  
Do not modify the original log; only read from it.  
Feel free to inspect the log to confirm the counts before writing the report.

Verification expectations after task completion:

FAILED 4
